summary: The concept of "sparrow foot marks" has no basis in recognized forensic science, as evidenced by their complete absence in available literature related to post-accident autopsies or driver identification. Since other methods—such as injury patterns, position within vehicle seats, or blood distribution—are clinically validated, the use of unverified markers like sparrow foot marks fails to meet diagnostic standards.
